title: Catalog-metadata license decision (CC-BY-4.0 vs CC0 for factual fields)
context: The catalog's metadata is authored by contributors; it is not simply copied from sources. The legal theory (PLAN §7) holds that we publish independently-verified factual descriptors plus a citation, not the custodians' copyrighted prose. The open question is whether our own metadata should be licensed CC-BY-4.0 (requiring attribution) or CC0 (waiving all rights, maximizing interoperability). The distinction matters: purely factual fields (aggregate counts, access tier, rights booleans verified from public sources) may be best served by CC0 to maximize reuse; narrative/interpretive fields (rationale, completeness notes) may warrant CC-BY-4.0. This decision must be made before broad publishing begins so the outputLicense field in records is settled. The current default is CC-BY-4.0 with upstream attribution preserved.
objective: Record a written, dated governance decision on the catalog-metadata license (CC-BY-4.0 vs CC0 for purely factual fields vs hybrid), grounded in the legal theory for independently-verified factual descriptors, and apply it consistently to the outputLicense field in all catalog records.
acceptanceCriteria: A committed policy document (policy-lic-035.md) records the governance decision: CC-BY-4.0 for all metadata, CC0 for purely factual fields, or a hybrid approach; the decision is dated and signed off by the Maintainer.
The document explains the legal theory (independently-verified factual descriptors + citation, not the custodian's prose) and why the chosen license applies.
If a hybrid approach is chosen, the document defines exactly which fields are CC0 (purely factual) and which are CC-BY-4.0 (interpretive/narrative), and how this is serialized in the record schema.
The decision is applied consistently across all catalog records' outputLicense fields; records generated before this decision are updated.
Output document licensed CC-BY-4.0 (the policy document itself); DCO signed-off.
resources: open-cohort-catalog PLAN.md §7 (legal theory for our metadata; CC-BY vs CC0 open question)
Creative Commons CC-BY-4.0 and CC0 1.0 Universal deed summaries
output: A committed policy document (policy-lic-035.md) recording the governance decision on catalog-metadata licensing (CC-BY-4.0 vs CC0), with rationale and the field-level application rule, settling the outputLicense default for all catalog records.
